title: Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1986
summary: Federal anti-drug law that established harsh mandatory minimum sentences, including the 100-to-1 crack versus powder cocaine sentencing disparity.
outcome_summary: Contributed to major racial disparities in federal sentencing and incarceration, especially affecting Black communities.
impact_notes: A major driver of sentencing disparities tied to the War on Drugs.

scores.notes: Major harmful criminal justice policy with long-lasting effects.

title: Anti-Drug Abuse Act of 1988
summary: Expanded the federal drug war framework, created the Office of National Drug Control Policy, and intensified criminal penalties tied to drug enforcement.
outcome_summary: Deepened punitive drug enforcement and reinforced policies that disproportionately affected Black communities.
impact_notes: Extended the punitive direction of 1980s federal drug policy.

title: Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994
summary: Large federal crime law that expanded policing, prisons, and sentencing measures while also including provisions like the Violence Against Women Act.
outcome_summary: Contributed to a tougher federal crime environment that many critics argue worsened mass incarceration and disproportionately harmed Black communities.
impact_notes: Included both harmful punitive expansion and some provisions viewed as beneficial, making it best classified as mixed.

scores.notes: Large mixed-impact law with major long-term criminal justice effects.

sources.source_title: Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act of 1994

title: Fair Sentencing Act of 2010
summary: Reduced the federal sentencing disparity between crack and powder cocaine offenses.
outcome_summary: Reduced one of the most notorious federal sentencing disparities affecting Black defendants, though it did not eliminate it entirely.
impact_notes: Important sentencing reform with incomplete but meaningful corrective effect.

scores.notes: Important reform of crack sentencing disparities.

title: First Step Act
summary: Federal criminal justice reform law that reduced some sentencing disparities and expanded earned-time credits and reentry support.
outcome_summary: Provided meaningful but partial federal sentencing and prison reform, with particular importance for communities harmed by prior drug sentencing rules.
impact_notes: A meaningful reform law, though limited relative to the scale of the broader system.

scores.notes: Useful but partial criminal justice reform.
